segments.text: Hello, everyone. My name is Vladimir Vladokov-Lazov. I'm the CTO at Kero Software, and today I'm going to talk about AI for architectural visualization,
and how the work that we're doing can help architects and our users in general go from concept to finished image literally in a matter of minutes with the use of generative.

How does AI rendering differ from traditional rendering that we've had until a few years ago?

So traditionally you start with a 3D model that is developing some kind of platform like Revit or 3DS Max, then you enhance that model by adding furniture, vegetation, you apply all different materials for the surfaces in the scene, set up the lighting, set up the environment, render the final image and then to what the other post vacuum is necessary to get a good results.
This is a log in this process that could take multiple days and multiple revisions to get right, which AI rendering the second half of the project could be greatly accelerated.
So you can start with the 3D model and through a simple text prompt along with a few reference images you can get to final result, basically in a matter of minutes.
The extreme version of this is where you don't really have a model but you just have a sketch in mind or idea in mind of a building or another project and you can turn this into a photo real image.
Again, literally in a matter of minutes without having to model your render, but you can present a concept very, very quickly.

Now let's focus a little bit on how AI can be used to enhance final renders this is not for concept renders anymore this is when you already have a quite detailed 3d model and you want to improve aspects of it which aspects specifically well in our case.

This is people and vegetation because they are the hardest to render in a photorealistic way especially CG people are very complicated and and while it is possible to create photorealistic digital versions of humans it's typically a lot of process and in the context of architecture visualization is usually not enough time to do that or at least hasn't there hasn't been a way to listen to now.

Vegetations similar similar situation models typically have very high level of detail lots of little pieces of geometry like leaves and flowers and so on which typically very slow to render in a photoreal way.

Other things that we could do is adding perfection to large surfaces adding adding details to walls or floors and things like that which typically would take a lot of time to do in 3d can be done now quite easily with AI.
We can also enhance individual objects while preserving the rest of the image.

Other interesting uses for AI as a post process enhancing renders up to up to four times increasing the resolution which saves render time up to 16 times now all of this is part of our collaboration service so this is not at the moment done inside.
Host applications like ready to 3ds max but there is an easy way to go from those applications to our collaboration service and use.

Use all these features so in this example here we are focusing on the AI in cancer way we have uploaded a render from our escape render engine and it's a okay render but the 3d model and the trees are kind of okay for that level but not super photorealistic.

So our global enhancement algorithm automatically affects people in vegetation and makes them more realistic so this is a close up of the person and if we slip between the enhanced and the original version we can see that the person's face and clothes and everything is much better as well as vegetation in the background has a lot of added detail and overall the look image looks a lot more photorealistic but at the same time the actual building and the background is a lot more.
The actual building and the actual design everything is exactly the same which was a very important requirement for for the system.

You can also have some more targeted enhancement for people specifically oftentimes you need to depict a building or project that is specific to certain geographic locations so people have to look a certain way or they have to be dressed in a certain way.
And having a large library that represents all of these models or finding these 3D models online could be challenging but there is a way to do this as a post process through generative AI.
So in our example here you can select the face of the person you can specify you can make them older you can make them younger, change the hair color and so on.
So it's quite useful actually so in this case we could go from a middle-aged person to an older person to a person with a white hair quite easily.
And all of these things do not require any rebranders of the image of this can be done as a post process.
The large service in Hasmont works by allowing you to add some more details or imperfections to surface.
In my example here I was going for the wall that we have here on the buildings or wanted it to look damaged and it kind of managed to do this quite easily.
So if I flip between the images you can kind of see what this looks like.
And finally if you are happy with the result from a render of you can upscale it up to four times.
This is very useful because rendering at the original resolution can be very very slow so it can take minutes to hours.

If you use an offline render but with our image upscaling feature you can save quite a bit of render time just by upscaling this in post and the way this works was you upload an image to our cloud collaboration service you have the option to upscale it and you can choose the resolution to upscale to where it's two times or four times.
And automatically details where it's needed on this case you can see the difference here between the original render and the result also super useful and saves a lot of time.
And now a few words about the future and where we are looking at sometimes abusing AI.

Ideally we want to go beyond just images and videos those are useful but ultimately we are working with the 3D scene and we want to be able to go back from from the nice photo real render that we generated with Ferris we would like to go back to the 3D world and have this as a 3D scene.
This is useful for a number of reasons first a 3D scene can be now again in real time or in virtual reality.

You can also directly rearrange it anyway that you want you can do also simulations we have a module call in impact that can tell you what's the thermal comfort level of a building we will be adding daylight analysis to that and other simulation features and
they all work in 3D so going being able to go back from 30 to 3D or feel will be very valuable.

Other things that would be useful to do is at the moment you just get a static image or single video but it will be very interesting if what to get out of generative AI is a full of all three environment that you can navigate and you can explore.
Even VR or just as a game level style navigation this could make the experience a lot more interactive so this is also something that we are looking into and on a more mundane aspect.
At the end of your project when you need to produce construction documents it's all of tedious work that we feel can be to large extent automated so AI.
In all these things are aspects that we are working on.
You're also exploring generating 3D elements of buildings like lighting fixtures and air ducts.
We're not at a place where AI can generate a full building by itself but elements of that building could be potentially generated much faster using AI.

Landscaping is also an area that can be improved based on the geographical location and the time of the year and the system will be able to suggest possible plant species to use what they how they should be placed and it could give you some suggestion.

Also one thing that we have noticed as a potential use for AI is that architects sometimes struggle to find good camera angles to present their projects and this is maybe something that AI can help with.

Photographers can have very specific knowledge on how to do this in the best possible way and while AI can replace real photographers it can still suggest good camera angles and good ways to present a project.
It can also help with setting up the mood of the scene suitable assets to use for vegetation for furniture and all kinds of useful stuff.
What I show today is mostly 2D generation images and videos but the next step and what I hope to be able to show next year is how AI can be used to manipulate 3D scenes.
And that was everything from me for today.
